title: Samadera pentapetala
taxonomy: Samadera pentapetala is classified in the family Sapindaceae. This species belongs to the genus Samadera, which is part of the order Sapindales. The classification can be broken down as follows: Kingdom - Plantae, Clade - Angiosperms, Clade - Eudicots, Clade - Rosids, Order - Sapindales, Family - Sapindaceae, Genus - Samadera, and Species - S. pentapetala. The Sapindaceae family comprises of around 140 genera of trees and shrubs. The classification is an essential tool for understanding the evolutionary relationships among different species.
uses: Samadera pentapetala has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The plant is also used to treat various gastrointestinal problems, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers. Its leaves are used to treat various skin conditions, including eczema and dermatitis. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing the risk of certain diseases.
